author: deme1744
content:
I've been adding these test vectors also to our testing for Nimbus EL.
So far this added testing does:
- the blocks get executed stateful (like any other eest vectors)
- witness is build as blocks are executed stateful
- stateless execution is ran with created witness (this is currently in same binary, ran after stateful execution, so it is not really a proper separate guest program, however same code is used)
- the build witness is compared with witness in test vector (the not-serialized version, need to add the part still for serialization to use the "raw" input / output and compare)
 
Now I noticed that between `v0.3.2` and `v0.3.3` quite a bit more test cases were added than these mentioned in release description.

Looking at the differences, I assume a lot of them come from simply the fact that the amount of BAL test vectors got increased probably + I also see a whole added `ported_static` folder.
Trying to understand how these test vectors work, I assume it builds on the existing ones by adding witness data and then `eip8025_optional_proofs` are specifically added to test witness and edge cases of witness? Is that right?

author: deme1744
content:
Current state for nimbus is:
For `v0.3.2` : `[Summary] 14128 tests run (34.9s): 14104 OK, 24 FAILED, 0 SKIPPED`
For `v0.3.3`: `[Summary] 22717 tests run (68.8s): 22501 OK, 216 FAILED, 0 SKIPPED`

Note that not all failures are fails due to a difference in witness data.
In some case we still have issues with generation of the data. And in some other cases our state root differs after stateless execution.

author: jsign
content:
> Trying to understand how these test vectors work, I assume it builds on the existing ones by adding witness data and then eip8025_optional_proofs are specifically added to test witness and edge cases of witness? Is that right?

Yep exactly. `eip8025_optional_proofs` are specially crafted cases relevant for execution witness, as to have more minimal test cases to test important angles. I usually only mention these in the release description, since hte rest comes from the rebased lastest BAL-devnet branch.
On each release, we also get "for free" probably many more tests from other tests added (e.g. new BAL tests), since generate the execution witness for all other tests too.

(So basically all "yes" to what you said)

author: jsign
content: Wow this is great news -- this looks quite good
